description: Phase 7 — Beach Detail View. Build the beach detail screen with current score, pros/cons reasons inside a new surf-disclosure Lit component, MapLibre map, 10-day forecast strip, favourite toggle, and staleness indicator.

userStories.title: Extend beachStore with selectedBeach + fetchBeachDetail
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the beach store to manage the currently viewed beach so the detail page has a single source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/types/beach.ts extended with HourlyForecast interface { timestamp: string, rawData: Record<string, number>, surfScore: number, label: string, reasons: string[], confidence: number }
ui/src/types/beach.ts extended with DailySummary interface { date: string, bestWindowStart: string | null, bestWindowEnd: string | null, peakScore: number, overallLabel: string }
ui/src/types/beach.ts extended with ForecastSnapshot interface { beachId: string, fetchedAt: string, hourlyForecasts: HourlyForecast[], dailySummaries: DailySummary[] }
beachStore state extended with: selectedBeach (Beach | null), selectedForecast (ForecastSnapshot | null), detailLoading (boolean), detailError (string | null)

New action fetchBeachDetail(id: string): if state.beaches contains the beach, set selectedBeach from cache immediately; then fetches GET http://localhost:3000/beaches/${id} and GET http://localhost:3000/beaches/${id}/forecast in parallel via Promise.all; sets selectedBeach and selectedForecast on success; sets detailError on failure; manages detailLoading flag
New action clearSelectedBeach(): resets selectedBeach and selectedForecast to null
Typecheck passes (cd ui && yarn typecheck)
userStories.notes: API_BASE constant already exists at the top of beachStore.ts — reuse it. Use Options API style defineStore (matches Phase 6 store).

userStories.title: Add /beaches/:id route and BeachDetailView scaffold
userStories.description: As a user, I want tapping a beach card to open a dedicated detail page so I can see more information about that beach.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/router.ts updated with route { path: '/beaches/:id', name: 'beach-detail', component: () => import('../views/BeachDetailView.vue') }
ui/src/views/BeachDetailView.vue created

Page header bar contains a back button (<surf-icon name='arrow-left'>) on the left that calls router.back() (or router.push('/') if window.history.length <= 1) and the beach name centered/left-aligned
HomeView.vue cards wired so clicking a <surf-card> calls router.push({ name: 'beach-detail', params: { id: beach.id } })
Page reads route.params.id and triggers beachStore.fetchBeachDetail(id) on mount
Page calls beachStore.clearSelectedBeach() in onBeforeUnmount
Loading state shows text/spinner while detailLoading is true
Error state shows detailError text plus a <surf-button> retry that calls fetchBeachDetail again
Layout is mobile-first, max-width 600px centered on desktop, var(--space-4) horizontal padding
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001 (fetchBeachDetail action). Existing surf-card already has clickable prop — use @click on the card element.

userStories.title: Hero block — name, score, favourite, staleness
userStories.description: As a user, I want the most important information (current score, freshness, favourite toggle) at the top of the detail page.
acceptanceCriteria: Below the page header bar, a hero block shows: beach name (--font-size-xl bold), region (--font-size-sm --color-text-secondary)
Large score display: score number in --font-size-2xl (or largest available token) next to a <surf-badge> with the currentLabel as variant

Favourite heart button (<surf-icon name='heart'>) positioned in the top-right of the hero block, ≥44x44px touch target, calls beachStore.toggleFavourite(selectedBeach.id), filled red (--color-surf-poor) when in beachStore.favourites
Staleness line under the score uses formatRelativeTime(lastUpdated) from ui/src/utils/time.ts (already exists from Phase 6) and turns --color-surf-maybe if older than 6 hours
If currentScore === null, show <surf-badge variant='neutral'>No data</surf-badge> and 'No data yet' instead of the score number and staleness
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reuse formatRelativeTime and the favourites pattern from Phase 6 HomeView. The 6-hour warning logic is identical to the home view card staleness.

userStories.title: Build surf-disclosure Lit component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a reusable expand/collapse block component so the detail view (and future views) can hide secondary content behind a tap.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/components/lit/SurfDisclosure.ts created and registered as <surf-disclosure>
Use Lit declare + constructor pattern for reactive properties (matches existing Lit components in this repo)
Properties: summary (string, default ''), open (boolean, default false)
Default slot renders the collapsible body content
Named slot 'summary' allows rich header content; when present, overrides the summary string property
Header row shows the summary text plus a <surf-icon name='chevron-right'> that rotates 90° when open via CSS transform transition
Clicking the header toggles open and dispatches a CustomEvent('toggle', { detail: { open }, bubbles: true, composed: true })
Body uses max-height + opacity transition (~200ms) for smooth open/close animation
Header has min-height 44px touch target, --space-3 padding, --font-size-md text
Component imported once in ui/src/main.ts (matches existing Lit component registration pattern)
Component added to /components dev gallery view with at least one closed example and one open example with sample body content
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Match the file/folder convention of other Lit components in ui/src/components/lit/. The chevron rotation: transform: rotate(90deg) when open. Use ::slotted selector to style slotted content if needed.

userStories.title: Reasons displayed as pros/cons inside disclosure
userStories.description: As a user, I want to understand why a beach has its current score by seeing what's working for and against the conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/utils/reasons.ts created with classifyReason(reason: string, score: number): 'pro' | 'con' — pros: reason starts with '+' OR contains 'ideal'/'good'/'favourable' (case-insensitive); cons: starts with '-' OR contains 'too'/'wrong'/'off'/'weak'; default: 'pro' if score >= 50 else 'con'
Reasons sourced from selectedForecast.hourlyForecasts[0].reasons (current hour); empty array if no forecast
BeachDetailView renders <surf-disclosure summary='Why this score'> beneath the hero block
Inside the disclosure, two grouped lists: 'Working for you' (pros) and 'Working against you' (cons)

Each list item shows a leading icon + reason text in a row with --space-2 gap; pros use ✓ in --color-surf-very-good, cons use ✕ in --color-surf-poor (unicode characters wrapped in styled spans are acceptable)
If a group is empty, hide its heading and list entirely
If there are no reasons at all, the disclosure body shows 'No detailed reasons available' in --color-text-secondary
Disclosure starts collapsed by default
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (surf-disclosure component). Unicode ✓/✕ is preferred over extending the icon registry to keep this story small.

userStories.title: Install MapLibre and render BeachMap component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see where the beach is on a map with the surrounding coastline so I have geographic context.
acceptanceCriteria: maplibre-gl installed via yarn add maplibre-gl in ui/
maplibre-gl/dist/maplibre-gl.css imported once in ui/src/main.ts
ui/src/components/BeachMap.vue created — Vue SFC with props coords ({ lat: number, lon: number }) and name (string)
On mount, initialises a MapLibre map using the demo style 'https://demotiles.maplibre.org/style.json' (with a code comment noting this is MVP-only and to swap in Phase 13)
Map centers on the beach coords with zoom 12
A single Marker is placed at the beach coords with a Popup showing the beach name on click
Map container has fixed height 240px, full-width, border-radius var(--radius-md) (or --space-2 if --radius-md not defined)
Map instance is destroyed via map.remove() in onBeforeUnmount
If map initialisation throws, show 'Map unavailable' text in --color-text-secondary instead
BeachDetailView renders <BeachMap :coords='selectedBeach.coords' :name='selectedBeach.name' /> between the disclosure block and the forecast strip
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: BeachDetailView is lazy-loaded via dynamic import in router so MapLibre's ~200KB doesn't bloat the home view. Use a template ref to give MapLibre its container element.

userStories.title: 10-day horizontal forecast strip
userStories.description: As a user, I want to scan the next 10 days of conditions at a glance so I can plan ahead.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/utils/time.ts extended with formatWeekday(dateString: string): string (e.g. 'Mon') and formatDayOfMonth(dateString: string): string (e.g. '14')
ui/src/components/ForecastStrip.vue created — Vue SFC with prop dailySummaries: DailySummary[]
Container uses display: flex; overflow-x: auto; gap: var(--space-3); scroll-snap-type: x mandatory; -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;
Each day card uses <surf-card padding> with min-width 96px, scroll-snap-align: start

Each day card shows: weekday short label (--font-size-xs), day-of-month number (--font-size-md bold), <surf-badge> with peakScore as text and overallLabel as variant, best window time as 'HH:MM–HH:MM' in --font-size-xs --color-text-secondary
If a daily summary lacks bestWindowStart/bestWindowEnd, the card shows '—' in place of the time range
BeachDetailView renders <ForecastStrip :daily-summaries='selectedForecast.dailySummaries' /> beneath the BeachMap
Strip horizontal padding aligns with the page content padding so the first card edge matches the rest of the content
If selectedForecast is null or dailySummaries is empty, the strip is not rendered (or shows 'No forecast available')
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: bestWindowStart/bestWindowEnd in dailySummaries are stored as 'HH:MM' strings on the backend (per Phase 6 progress notes) — extract directly, no ISO parsing needed. Verify shape against backend response if uncertain.
